items.summary: Nigeria’s pioneer Naira-denominated Private Debt Fund, FCMB-TLG Private Debt Fund, records 103.43% subscription, deepening the pool of long-term local-currency capital available to mid-sized companies FCMB Asset Management Limited (FCMBAM) and TLG Capital today announced the successful close of the FCMB-TLG Private Debt Fund Series II Offer. items.title: Glover App announces orderly wind-down of operations
items.summary:
Glover App today announced its decision to commence an orderly wind-down of its operations, with trading on the platform scheduled to end on July 27, 2026, and official ceasing of operations on July 31, 2026. Founded in 2021, Glover reached over a million registered users in Nigeria and Ghana through its gift card trading, Airtime2Cash […]

items.title: Transport of Ebola victims’ bodies fuels Congo outbreak, UN agency warns
items.summary:
Movement of Ebola victims’ bodies across different parts of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is increasing the risk of further transmission as the country battles one of its worst outbreaks in recent years, a UN agency, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) has warned.

items.title: Tinubu signs Virtual Assets Executive Order, creates CBN-led council to harmonise crypto regulation
items.summary:
President Bola Tinubu has signed the Presidential Executive Order on Virtual Assets Coordination, 2026, establishing a new framework to coordinate the regulation of virtual assets across government agencies as Nigeria seeks to curb fraud while supporting innovation in the digital economy.

items.title: Coronation Infrastructure Fund to pay N789.8 million H1 dividend on July 28
items.summary:
The Coronation Infrastructure Fund (CIF) has announced a semi-annual income distribution of N8.985 per unit for the half-year ended June 30, 2026, amounting to approximately N789.78 million payout to the 87.9 million shares outstanding.

items.summary: BIC® South Africa is continuing its education drive across Gauteng, equipping learners with essential writing tools through its Pen Licence and Matric Pen Drop-off initiatives to support academic success.

items.title: Poultry Master Plan will provide roadmap to make Ghana self-sufficient – Dr Acquaye
items.summary: The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Agri-Impact Limited and the Happy Poultry Project, Dr Dan Acquaye, has said the development of a Poultry Master Plan is aimed at providing a clear roadmap to make Ghana self-sufficient in poultry production through a phased approach.

items.title: Nkoko Nkitinkiti needs a 10 to 20-year poultry strategy to succeed – Rockland Farms CEO
items.summary: The Chief Executive Officer of Rockland Farms, Edith Akosah Wheatland, has called for a long-term, legally backed roadmap to guide the implementation of the government's Nkoko Nkitinkiti initiative, saying that Ghana's poultry industry cannot be transformed through short-term programmes.

items.title: Tullow Oil Secures Additional $9 Million as It Revises Kenya Exit Agreement
items.summary: British oil producer Tullow Oil will receive an additional $9 million (about Ksh1.16 billion) after agreeing to revise the terms of its completed exit from Kenya, trading future royalty and participation rights for an immediate cash payment as it continues efforts to reduce debt and streamline its global operations. items.title: US introduces sweeping changes to student visas, caps stay at four years
items.summary:
The United States has overhauled its student visa framework, ending a long-standing policy that allowed international students to remain in the country for as long as they maintained their academic status. items.title: Nkoko Nkitinkiti introduced to cut poultry imports and boost local production – Demordzi
items.summary:
The National Coordinator of the Feed Ghana Programme, Bright Demordzi, says its Nkoko Nkitinkiti programme is a key import substitution intervention designed to increase domestic poultry production, create jobs and reduce Ghana’s dependence on imported chicken.

items.title: GAPFA was excluded from Feed Ghana policy formulation despite repeated requests – CEO
items.summary: The Chief Executive Officer of the Greater Accra Poultry Farmers Association (GAPFA), Ali Muhammed, has expressed disappointment over what he describes as the exclusion of the country's largest poultry farmers' association from the formulation of the Feed Ghana Programme policy, despite the association's extensive experience and investment in the poultry value chain.

items.title: Uber to acquire Glovo in Kenya, other markets in Sh1.95tn deal
items.summary: The transaction, valued at Sh1.95 trillion, covers businesses operating under the Glovo brand in Africa, Asia, Middle East and Latin America.

items.title: Veterinary Association calls for stronger collaboration to ensure success of Nkoko Nkitinkiti Programme
items.summary: A representative of the Ghana Veterinary Medical Association, Dr Kwao Pecku, has attributed the failure of successive government poultry policies to weak implementation, inadequate collaboration with key stakeholders, and poor animal health management systems.

items.summary:

items.summary: The Kenyan government is pushing ahead with a major nationwide programme to tag and digitally register millions of livestock, aiming to transform the country’s traditional herding sector into a competitive, traceable supply chain capable of accessing premium international markets. items.summary: The Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) has opened the 2026 inter-institutional transfer application window, giving students 30 days to seek placement in different universities, Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions and other recognised higher learning institutions. items.title: Insurers bundle investments with cover as uptake stalls
items.summary: Kenya's insurers are increasingly combining investment products with health and life cover to boost insurance uptake, which remains among the lowest globally.
